questions.question: A drought caused the water level in a lake to $\downarrow 15\%$. If the original water level was $200$ meters, what is the new water level?
The average temperature in July is $25^{\circ}$C. If it $\uparrow 8\%$ in August, what is the new average temperature?
A greenhouse gas $\uparrow 22\%$ from $1.8$ million tons to $?
If $35\%$ of the forest is covered by $\text{Coniferous Trees}$ and the forest is $2500$ acres, what is the area covered by $\text{Coniferous Trees}$?
A river's water level $\downarrow 12\%$. If the original water level was $1200$ meters, what is the decrease in water level?
A city's air quality $\uparrow 18\%$ after reducing emissions. If the original air quality index was $50$, what is the new air quality index?
If $28\%$ of a park is covered by $\text{Wetlands}$, and the park is $4000$ acres, what is the area not covered by $\text{Wetlands}$?
A region's glacier $\downarrow 9\%$ due to climate change. If the original glacier's area was $80$ square kilometers, what is the area of the glacier now?
A city's population $\uparrow 15\%$ in $5$ years. If the current population is $50000$, what is the expected population after $10$ years, assuming the same growth rate?

A company's $\text{Carbon Footprint}$ $\downarrow 20\%$ after implementing $\text{Renewable Energy}$ sources. If their original $\text{Carbon Footprint}$ was $5000$ tons per year, what is their new $\text{Carbon Footprint}$?

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of percentage calculations in real-world problems
Use visual aids to help students understand concept of percentage increase/decrease
Encourage students to use real-world examples from weather and environment to practice percentage calculations
